name: Kyōshirō Tōdō

Context

Kyōshirō Tōdō is known as "General Tōdō". A former elite officer of the Japanese army before the Britannian invasion, he is considered a national hero and a master strategist. He joins the Black Knights as the supreme commander of their military forces.

⋆。‧+°♱༺𓆩❦︎𓆪༻♱༉‧+ ̊.⋆。‧+°♱༺𓆩❦︎𓆪༻♱༉‧+ ̊.⋆。‧+°♱༺𓆩❦︎𓆪༻♱༉‧+ ̊.

Biography

Full Name: Kyōshirō Tōdō (藤堂 鏡志朗)

Age: Approximately 28-30 years old (at series start)

Nationality: Japanese (Number)

Rank: Former Japanese Army General, Military Commander of the Black Knights

Status: Alive

Personal History: Tōdō led the Japanese resistance during the initial invasion. Captured and imprisoned, he is freed by Zero. He joins the Black Knights along with his elite unit, the Four Holy Swords.

Personality

Tōdō is calm, serious, and stoic. He is a man of few words but possesses great military wisdom. He often disagrees with Zero's methods, preferring honor and strategy over deception. Although initially distrustful of Zero, he acknowledges his genius. He has a strong sense of justice.
